Empires & Puzzles Costumed Guan Yu Review: Inferno General Strategy Guide

The God of War has ascended. Costumed Guan Yu trades his classic robes for dragon-forged armor and a massive stat boost. Is he the ultimate answer to the minion meta? Our deep dive analyzes his new stats, skills, and why he's terrifying in Rush Wars.
Costumed Guan Yu Review: The Inferno General Burns Bright
Costumed Guan Yu: The Inferno General Awakes
Guan Yu was already a respectable hero, but his
reinvents him for the modern era. Released in late 2024, this upgrade transforms him from a niche counter into a stat-monster powerhouse. He swaps his Paladin class for
, picks up a massive stat boost, and gains the incredible "Unstoppable Minions" passive. If you thought the base version was annoying for minion users, this version is a nightmare.

The "Inferno General" title is well-earned. Gone are the calm green robes; in their place is intricate, dragon-scale armor with blue and gold detailing that screams "I have high stats." The visual design is more aggressive and ornate, reflecting his upgraded power level. He looks less like a historical general and more like a mythological deity ready to cleave a mountain in half.
The stats are where the magic happens.
, Costumed Guan Yu is statistically relevant in the 2026 meta. He can take a hit from modern snipers and live to tell the tale.
His stat distribution is beautifully aggressive, supporting his role as a "Hit-3" nuker.
He sits comfortably in the upper echelon of heroes, only slightly behind the absolute latest "Tower" or "Astral" heroes in raw power, but competitive enough to be a mainstay.
Special Skill: Green Dragon Inferno Blade
The "Inferno Blade" is a direct upgrade to the original "Crescent Blade."
A slight bump from 390%, but combined with his much higher attack stat, the actual damage output is massive. He hits like a truck.

is higher, and he reaches high numbers much faster. With just 3 enemy minions, he’s already at -45% defense down.

This turns Guan Yu into a surprisingly durable tanky hero if he has minion support, as his meat shields are guaranteed to stick around until destroyed by damage.

gives him a raw +20% attack boost for the first 6 turns. This is arguably better than "Special Boost" because it also enhances his tile damage, making him a titan-slaying machine and a tile-cascade threat in raids.
The costume revived a fading hero.
. He is a top-tier green option for Wars and Raids. His stats allow him to survive the "One-Shot" meta, and his skill punishes the still-prevalent minion meta (looking at you, Construct family).
He is a main-roster hero. If you have this costume, you level him immediately.
Base is a "Survivalist Debuffer" (Paladin). Costume is a "Heavy Hitter" (Barbarian).
Costume hits harder, debuffs harder (initially), and lasts longer.

The only downside is losing the Paladin talent. The Barbarian "Bleed" talent is weak (essentially negligible damage), whereas the Paladin defense boost was actually useful. However, the raw stat increase of the costume far outweighs the class talent loss.
. Always. The stat difference is too large to ignore.
As a Barbarian, his class talent (Bleed) is underwhelming, so ignore it for decision-making. Focus on stats.
Maximizes his Aether Power (Attack Up) and Special Skill damage.
Barbarians are naturally squishier than Paladins.

The best counter to the minion war effect.
High attack stat + Attack Up Aether + Defense Down = High Scores.
Liu Bei -> Costumed Guan Yu -> Hulda
Liu Bei and Hulda summon minions. Guan Yu's passive makes his minions unremovable. The enemy Grimble cries in the corner while Guan Yu shreds them.
Costumed Evelyn -> Costumed Guan Yu -> Costumed Lianna
The classic 1-2-3 punch. Evelyn strips Elemental Defense, Guan Yu strips regular Defense (and ignores minions), Lianna deletes whoever is left.
who resists passive effects, or heroes that block ailments entirely (Aramis).
None really, other than heroes who overwrite his defense down with a weaker one (don't fire Frigg
This is a jackpot pull. He will be your main green hero for a long time. Pair him with any healer and let him carry you through the map stages.
He is worth the Alpha Aethers. The stat boost from the costume plus the LB2 stats puts him in the "Elite" category. Use him in your War Defense or primary Green Stack.
"I’ve had Guan Yu since he was first released... The costume seems clearly better but the class is not great unfortunately." - A veteran player noting the Barbarian downgrade.
"My strongest color became even stronger." - Players generally agree that while the class swap is 'meh', the stats make him a monster.
"This is the way I went... Sword path." Most players are confirming that Attack path is the only way to go to keep him relevant as a nuker.

Costumed Guan Yu is what happens when you take a good hero and give them a modern gym membership. He hits harder, lasts longer, and shrugs off minion counters. While we mourn the loss of his Paladin shield, we celebrate the destruction he brings to the battlefield. He is the Inferno General, and business is booming.
An S-Tier update to a classic hero. Essential for modern rosters.
Costumed Guan Yu brings modern stats and burning power to the War of Three Kingdoms. We review his updated kit, Unstoppable Minions passive, and comparisons to the base form.
